frameworks.name: theory-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: structured causal world models serve as forward simulators for planning and generalization

frameworks.name: program synthesis
frameworks.one_line: synthesizes hierarchical theory programs as the world model representation

frameworks.name: bilevel planning
frameworks.one_line: hierarchical planner exploits abstractions grounded by a low-level transition model

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: standard RL contrasted for falling short of human sample efficiency

core_question: Can hierarchical theory representations and program synthesis make theory-based RL agents scale to large domains while preserving sample-efficient human-like learning?
key_assumptions: agents benefit from structured causal world models as forward simulators
general-purpose abstractions (e.g., 'move to') transfer across environments
hierarchical theory structure admits efficient bilevel planning
manipulations: grounding of abstractions through learned low-level transition model
key_findings: TheoryCoder learns and plans in larger domains than prior TBRL systems
hierarchical theory representations support more powerful generalization
program synthesis yields theories usable as forward simulators
interpretations: hierarchical theories plus program synthesis overcome TBRL scalability limits
general-purpose abstractions grounded by learned transitions enable human-like planning
limitations_noted: prior TBRL theory languages are restrictive
prior TBRL planning algorithms do not scale
